context.description: Implement complete Stripe backend integration: stripe_service.py for checkout session creation, payments route for per-letter purchase and webhook handling, subscriptions route for premium tier management. Idempotent webhook processing using stripe_webhook_events table.
acceptance_criteria: backend/services/stripe_service.py implements: create_checkout_session(user_id, price_id, metadata) -> session_url, create_subscription_checkout(user_id) -> session_url, construct_webhook_event(payload, sig_header) -> stripe.Event

backend/api/v1/routes/payments.py implements: POST /checkout (authenticated, creates Stripe checkout session for per-letter purchase), POST /webhook (raw body, validates Stripe signature, idempotent event processing via stripe_webhook_events table), GET /history (authenticated, returns user's payment history)

backend/api/v1/routes/subscriptions.py implements: POST /subscribe (creates Stripe subscription checkout), GET /status (returns current subscription_tier from profiles), POST /cancel (cancels Stripe subscription, downgrades to free)
Webhook events processed idempotently: INSERT INTO stripe_webhook_events ... ON CONFLICT (stripe_event_id) DO NOTHING
payment.succeeded webhook: INSERT INTO payments + UPDATE profiles SET subscription_tier='premium' (if subscription event)
Webhook endpoint returns HTTP 200 to Stripe within 2s (async processing)

Bundle pricing support: stripe_service.py includes STRIPE_PRICE_ID_BUNDLE config; POST /payments/bundle-checkout creates 3-letter bundle checkout session ($24 total); bundle purchase records as 3 separate payment credits in payments table
Verify with: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:8000/api/v1/payments/webhook (Stripe CLI)
implementation_approach: Step 1: Read backend/api/v1/router.py to understand how to register new routers
Step 2: Read backend/services/database_optimizer.py for upsert pattern to reuse for payments
Step 3: Create backend/services/stripe_service.py with Stripe client initialization from settings.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
Step 4: Create backend/api/v1/routes/payments.py with /checkout and /webhook endpoints
Step 6: Register both routers in backend/api/v1/router.py
Step 7: For /webhook, use Request body directly (not Pydantic) to preserve raw bytes for signature validation
CRITICAL: /webhook must NOT use verify_supabase_jwt dependency - Stripe calls it unauthenticated
pre_analysis: Read backend/api/v1/routes/tradelines.py - authenticated route pattern with Depends(verify_supabase_jwt)
